original_image_tags: A scanned page of aged, off-white, slightly translucent paper with punch holes at the top and ragged right edge. The page is primarily blank but shows ghosted, faint text from the reverse side or a page beneath it. The page contains several official stamps in red and black ink, including routing and indexing marks. The main visible content consists of the stamps at the bottom and the indexing labels at the top right.

elements.description: Page containing multiple stamps and handwritten annotations. A stamped 'EX-103', 'INDEXED-154', and 'RECORDED-154' are visible upside down in the upper right. A red rectangular stamp at the bottom contains the text 'MAILED 53' and 'JUL 10 1953' along with 'COMM - FBI'. A blue ink stamp in the lower middle reads 'RECEIVED MAIL ROOM', 'FBI', 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE', and 'JUL 10 5 24 PM '53'. A small red 'PAID' stamp is visible on the right edge. Faint, illegible text is visible through the back of the page.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual content of stamps and labels on the page, but Description B adds specific quoted text details (like 'MAILED 53', 'RECEIVED MAIL ROOM') that Description A omits without contradicting.
